summary: Hereditary hemorrhagic telangiectasia (HHT) causes pulmonar y arteriovenous malformations leading to bleeding into airways and painful respiration during exertion; it follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern due to genetic defects affecting TGF-beta signaling. This matches the presented case history closely.
